description: Fix SDK reference and guide documentation inaccuracies: add missing TypedPromptConfig interface definition, fix resource read() examples to include the uri parameter, and fix fetchJSON POST example to use postJSON.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add TypedPromptConfig interface definition to prompts.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the SDK prompts reference, I want to see the TypedPromptConfig interface definition so that I understand what properties the typed overload of definePrompt expects.
acceptanceCriteria: The TypedPromptConfig interface is defined in docs/content/docs/sdk/prompts.mdx, showing all its properties: name (string), description (string, optional), args (TArgs), arguments (PromptArgument[], optional), render (function returning Promise<PromptMessage[]> with typed args)
The interface definition appears near the definePrompt typed overload signature that references it (between the function signature block and the PromptDefinition section)
The definition uses a TypeScript code block consistent with how PromptDefinition and PromptArgument are already displayed on the same page
lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes

userStories.title: Fix resource read() examples to include uri parameter
userStories.description: As a plugin developer following the resources & prompts guide, I want the code examples to match the actual ResourceDefinition interface so that I write correct resource implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: The first resource example at docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx line 35 shows async read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent> instead of async read(): Promise<ResourceContent>
The typed resource example at line 136 also includes the uri parameter
The full plugin example resource at line 434 also includes the uri parameter
All three read() function bodies remain otherwise unchanged
lastUpdated frontmatter remains 2026-03-01 (already correct)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx

Three resource read() examples omit the uri parameter that the ResourceDefinition interface requires (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts line 73: read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent>).

Locations to fix:
1. Line 35: `async read(): Promise<ResourceContent>` → `async read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent>`
2. Line 136: `async read(): Promise<ResourceContent>` → `async read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent>`
3. Line 434: `async read()` → `async read(uri: string)`

The function bodies do NOT need to use the uri parameter — just add it to the signature. TypeScript allows ignoring parameters, and the existing examples hardcode the URI in the return value. But the signature should match the interface for accuracy.

Note: The text at line 50 already says 'The read() handler receives this URI' which is correct — only the examples are inconsistent.

userStories.title: Fix fetchJSON POST example to use postJSON
userStories.description: As a plugin developer following the full plugin example, I want the code example to use the correct SDK function for POST requests so that my requests include the required Content-Type header.
acceptanceCriteria: The createItem tool handle() function in docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx (around line 484-494) uses postJSON instead of fetchJSON with method: 'POST'
The postJSON call correctly passes (url, body, init?, schema?) — the body object is passed directly (not JSON.stringify'd), matching postJSON's API which handles serialization internally
The import block at line 416-424 already includes postJSON (no change needed since fetchJSON is still used elsewhere in the file)
lastUpdated frontmatter remains 2026-03-01 (already correct)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx

The createItem tool at lines 484-488 uses fetchJSON for a POST request:

```
const item = await fetchJSON<{ id: string; title: string; url: string }>('/api/items', {
  method: 'POST',
  body: JSON.stringify(params),
});
```

This is wrong for two reasons:
1. fetchJSON does NOT set Content-Type: application/json — only postJSON does (platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts postJSON sets headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' })
2. The guide itself (docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx line 258) recommends using postJSON for POST operations

Replace with:
```
const item = await postJSON<{ id: string; title: string; url: string }>('/api/items', params);
```

Note: postJSON takes (url, body, init?, schema?) where body is the raw object — it calls JSON.stringify internally. So pass `params` directly, not `JSON.stringify(params)`.

Also check the import block at line 416-424 — it imports `fetchJSON` but not `postJSON`. Both are needed: fetchJSON is used by the resource read() functions, and postJSON is needed for the tool. Add `postJSON` to the import if not already there.
